review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-118-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Deliverable-type classification relies on order-dependent substring matching
findings.evidence: deliverableTypeFor() classifies via brittle checks (title.includes("fixture"), includes("Hermes"), includes("test")) whose result depends on condition ordering; e.g. "Hermes fixture evidence" must hit the Hermes branch before the fixture branch, and "Claude missing-test prompt" must hit the exact-match test branch before the Claude branch. Verified correct for the current frozen title lists (counts resolve exactly to ui:35, claude_review:14, security_audit:12, implementation:37, fixture:36, test:10, hermes_evidence:6 = 150).
findings.recommendation: Accept as-is for this frozen catalog; if titles are extended in CP00-119+, prefer an explicit title→type map over substring heuristics to avoid silent miscounts.

findings.title: No-write / no-leak flags on matrix profiles are structurally forced, so related assertions are attestations not behavioral tests
findings.evidence: freezeFixtureProfile() unconditionally overrides unauthorized_count_exposed, hidden_field_names_exposed, executes_ai_retrieval, writes_product_state, etc. to false after spreading the input, so the validator/test loops checking these flags cannot fail by construction.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for a metadata-only synthetic catalog with no runtime behavior to exercise; note that leak/write protection is asserted at the contract level here and must be re-verified behaviorally when real UI/runtime surfaces land in a later pack.

parsed_review.rationale: The pack is a pure, frozen, metadata-only catalog with no runtime UI/API routes, no policy/audit/product/database writes, no export/share, and no AI/analytics execution. All 150 units, the 85/65 split, the 10 micro-phase coverage, every hard-coded deliverable count, the CP117 three-state inheritance, the blocked cross-tenant and AI/analytics cases, leak protections, and the CP00-119/RP02.P05.M04.S08 handoff reconcile exactly between the implementation, the JSON contract, the tests, and the standalone validator. The only findings are two P3 maintainability/attestation notes that do not affect correctness or boundaries. Nothing blocks closeout.
